title: Dichapetalum toxicarium
intro: Dichapetalum toxicarium, also known as the poison apple or monkey apple, is a plant species native to tropical Africa that belongs to the Dichapetalaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Dichapetalum toxicarium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Dichapetalum toxicarium belongs to the family Celastraceae. It is a genus of plant that is native to tropical Africa.
